text: The Parable of the Persistent Widow

segments.text: Then Jesus told them a parable about their need to pray at all times and not lose heart:
In a certain town there was a judge who neither feared God nor respected men.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And there was a widow in that town who kept appealing to him, ‘Give me justice against my adversary.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For a while he refused, but later he said to himself, ‘Though I neither fear God nor respect men,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: yet because this widow keeps pestering me, I will give her justice. Then she will stop wearing me out with her perpetual requests.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And the Lord said,
Listen to the words of the unjust judge.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Will not God bring about justice for His elect who cry out to Him day and night? Will He continue to defer their help?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I tell you, He will promptly carry out justice on their behalf. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will He find faith on earth?
segments.speaker: the Lord

text: The Pharisee and the Tax Collector

segments.text: To some who trusted in their own righteousness and viewed others with contempt, He also told this parable:
Two men went up to the temple to pray. One was a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: The Pharisee stood by himself and prayed,[^95]‘God, I thank You that I am not like the other men—swindlers, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I fast twice a week and pay tithes of all that I acquire.’
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: But the tax collector stood at a distance, unwilling even to lift up his eyes to heaven. Instead, he beat his breast and said, ‘God, have mercy on me, a sinner!’
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I tell you, this man, rather than the Pharisee, went home justified. For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but the one who humbles himself will be exalted.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Now people were even bringing their babies to Jesus for Him to place His hands on them. And when the disciples saw this, they rebuked those who brought them.
But Jesus called the children to Him and said,
Let the little children come to Me, and do not hinder them! For the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Truly I tell you, anyone who does not receive the kingdom of God like a little child will never enter it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then a certain ruler asked Him,
Good Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?
segments.speaker: certain ruler

segments.text: Why do you call Me good?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied.
No one is good except God alone.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You know the commandments: ‘Do not commit adultery, do not murder, do not steal, do not bear false witness, honor your father and mother.’[^96]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: All these I have kept from my youth,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he said.
On hearing this, Jesus told him,
You still lack one thing: Sell everything you own and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But when the ruler heard this, he became very sad, because he was extremely wealthy.
Seeing the man’s sadness,[^97] Jesus said,
How hard it is for the rich to enter the kingdom of God!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Indeed, it is easier for a camel to pass through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Those who heard this asked,
Who then can be saved?
segments.speaker: Those who heard this

segments.text: But Jesus said,
What is impossible with man is possible with God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Look,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: said Peter,
we have left all we had[^98] to follow You.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Truly I tell you,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied,
no one who has left home or wife or brothers or parents or children for the sake of the kingdom of God
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: will fail to receive many times more in this age—and in the age to come, eternal life.
segments.speaker: Jesus

text: The Third Prediction of the Passion

segments.text: Then Jesus took the Twelve aside and said to them,
Look, we are going up to Jerusalem, and everything the prophets have written about the Son of Man will be fulfilled.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He will be delivered over to the Gentiles and will be mocked and insulted and spit upon.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They will flog Him and kill Him, and on the third day He will rise again.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But the disciples did not understand any of these things. The meaning was hidden from them, and they did not comprehend what He was saying.
As Jesus drew near to Jericho, a blind man was sitting beside the road, begging.
When he heard the crowd going by, he asked what was happening.
Jesus of Nazareth is passing by,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they told him.
So he called out,
Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Those who led the way admonished him to be silent, but he cried out all the louder,
Son of David, have mercy on me!
segments.speaker: Those who led the way

segments.text: Jesus stopped and directed that the man be brought to Him. When he had been brought near, Jesus asked him,
What do you want Me to do for you?
segments.speaker: Those who led the way

segments.text: Lord,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he said,
let me see again.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Receive your sight!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied.
Your faith has healed you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Immediately he received his sight and followed Jesus, glorifying God. And all the people who saw this gave praise to God.
